appraisal_events.evidence: Il « se donnait l'air… de ne pas tenir aux deux jeunes gens » et, « désireux de faire briller sa verve insolente », profita de la situation pour lancer un « triomphal couplet ».
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator shows Charlus orchestrating the scene (feigned indifference to the sons, moving to another room) to display his superiority of mind and shine in society. This staging increases his rhetorical position, even if the tone remains slightly ironic.

status_effects.explanation: He emerges strengthened as a social performer and dominant mind of the scene.
